reasoning_model_1: Model 1 generated a male voice with high content accuracy and good fluency. However, regarding the core instruction to 'start with a bright tone quality and gradually transform into a thick/rich tone', the performance was poor. The voice starts with a relatively deep, chesty, and resonant timbre right from the beginning (00:00-00:05), which contradicts the instruction to start 'bright' (typically lighter, clearer, higher resonance). Throughout the audio, the timbre remains consistent with very little variation. While the delivery of the second sentence (00:06-00:09) is slightly more emphatic, it does not demonstrate a perceptible shift in 'sound quality' (音质) from bright to thick. The dynamic adjustment is essentially absent.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 generated a female voice with perfect content accuracy and natural fluency. Regarding the core paralinguistic instruction, the model performed excellently. The first part of the speech (00:00-00:05), covering the music festival, is delivered with a higher pitch, faster pace, and a lighter, 'brighter' tone, matching the excitement of the content. As the text transitions to the philosophical reflection (00:06-00:10), there is a distinct and natural shift: the pitch drops, the pace slows, and the voice adopts a more grounded, chesty resonance, effectively conveying the 'thick/heavy' (浑厚) quality requested. The transition aligns perfectly with the semantic shift.
model_comparison: This comparison centers on the ability to perform dynamic adjustment of voice quality (timbre) within a single generation. Model 1 (T1) failed to execute the specific requirements; it utilized a static, deep male voice throughout, missing the 'bright' starting point and showing no significant transition to a 'thick' tone (as it was thick to begin with). Model 2 (T2), conversely, demonstrated a clear and effective contrast. It started with a lighter, brighter delivery and successfully transitioned to a deeper, weightier tone for the second half of the sentence. T2's ability to modulate paralinguistic features to match the instruction makes it the clear winner.
